segments.text: And it came to pass, when Samuel was old, that he made his sons judges over Israel.
Now the name of his firstborn was Joel; and the name of his second, Abiah: they were judges in Beer-sheba.
And his sons walked not in his ways, but turned aside after lucre, and took bribes, and perverted judgment.
Then all the elders of Israel gathered themselves together, and came to Samuel unto Ramah,
And said unto him,
Behold, thou art old, and thy sons walk not in thy ways: now make us a king to judge us like all the nations.
segments.speaker: Elders of Israel

segments.text: But the thing displeased Samuel, when they said, Give us a king to judge us.
And Samuel prayed unto the LORD.
segments.speaker: Samuel

footnotes: displeased: Heb. was evil in the eyes of
heading: Israel Demands a King

segments.text: Hearken unto the voice of the people in all that they say unto thee: for they have not rejected thee, but they have rejected me, that I should not reign over them.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: According to all the works which they have done since the day that I brought them up out of Egypt even unto this day, wherewith they have forsaken me, and served other gods, so do they also unto thee.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Now therefore hearken unto their voice: howbeit yet protest solemnly unto them, and shew them the manner of the king that shall reign over them.
segments.speaker: God

footnotes: howbeit…: or, notwithstanding when thou hast solemnly protested against them then thou shalt

segments.text: And Samuel told all the words of the LORD unto the people that asked of him a king.
And he said,
This will be the manner of the king that shall reign over you: He will take your sons, and appoint them for himself, for his chariots, and to be his horsemen; and some shall run before his chariots.
segments.speaker: Samuel

segments.text: And he will appoint him captains over thousands, and captains over fifties; and will set them to ear his ground, and to reap his harvest, and to make his instruments of war, and instruments of his chariots.
segments.speaker: Samuel

segments.text: And he will take your daughters to be confectionaries, and to be cooks, and to be bakers.
segments.speaker: Samuel

segments.text: And he will take your fields, and your vineyards, and your oliveyards, even the best of them, and give them to his servants.
segments.speaker: Samuel

segments.text: And he will take the tenth of your seed, and of your vineyards, and give to his officers, and to his servants.
segments.speaker: Samuel

segments.text: And he will take your menservants, and your maidservants, and your goodliest young men, and your asses, and put them to his work.
segments.speaker: Samuel

segments.text: He will take the tenth of your sheep: and ye shall be his servants.
segments.speaker: Samuel

segments.text: And ye shall cry out in that day because of your king which ye shall have chosen you; and the LORD will not hear you in that day.
segments.speaker: Samuel

segments.text: Nevertheless the people refused to obey the voice of Samuel;
Nay; but we will have a king over us;
segments.speaker: People of Israel

segments.text: That we also may be like all the nations; and that our king may judge us, and go out before us, and fight our battles.
segments.speaker: People of Israel

segments.text: And Samuel heard all the words of the people, and he rehearsed them in the ears of the LORD.
Hearken unto their voice, and make them a king.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: And Samuel said unto the men of Israel,
Go ye every man unto his city.
segments.speaker: Samuel
